notes: Eval prompts use neutral user requests that do not mention the skill by name. Evals 7 (data-model-changes) and 8 (concurrency-state) cover those categories. Assertions for evals 5 and 6 require exact markers and terminal format.
6 of 8 evals discriminate (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6). 2 are non-discriminating (7, 8): baselines independently produce correct content including <!-- pr-human-guide --> markers for those scenarios.

The discriminating evals test format-specific behavior: exact HTML comment markers (evals 1, 2, 3, 4, 5), SHA-256 diff anchor links (eval 1), and the exact 'Review guide updated on PR #...' terminal format (eval 6).
with_skill runs are slower (+21s) and heavier (+5517 tokens) than baseline because they read SKILL.md and categories.md before analysis. The quality gain (+39% pass rate) justifies the cost.
